note: Domain Access is a submodule of Domain (light-documented): per-domain access control for content and users. These are recipe (easy) cases only, graded on response text; no site changes.

evals.prompt: The Domain Access module adds fields to content (and users) to control which domains an item belongs to. Name the two fields it adds and what each does.
evals.expected_output: field_domain_access (entity-reference to domain, cardinality -1: the domains the item is assigned to) and field_domain_all_affiliates (boolean: publish to all domains / affiliates).

evals.prompt: How does Domain Access let a site give an editor rights on only their assigned domains rather than site-wide? Name a couple of the per-domain permissions it defines.
evals.expected_output: It defines per-domain permissions such as 'create domain content', 'edit domain content', 'delete domain content', 'view unpublished domain content', 'publish to any assigned domain', and 'assign domain editors', plus per-bundle 'create/update/delete <type> content on assigned domains' — granted relative to the user's assigned domains, not site-wide.

evals.prompt: Which config object and settings form control Domain Access behaviour (such as putting the domain field on the node advanced tab), and at what admin path is the form?
evals.expected_output: The domain_access.settings config object (route domain_access.settings at /admin/config/domain/domain_access); keys include node_advanced_tab, node_advanced_tab_open, access_fields_removal, per_bundle_grants.
